evals.name: recall-forgotten-skill
evals.prompt: i had a skill that writes those opinionated design briefs for ui rebuilds, the one that helps you not end up with another generic shadcn dashboard. forgot the name. what was it called?
evals.expected_output: Names ui-brief specifically, in code formatting, with one line on what it does and how to trigger it. Doesn't list a bunch of unrelated skills.

evals.name: catalog-by-category
evals.prompt: give me a quick rundown of all my skills grouped by what they do — i want to actually see the catalog instead of guessing names every time
evals.expected_output: A grouped list (orchestration / roles / contracts / meta / git / workflows or similar), one short line per skill, scannable. Includes plugin skills only if it separates them clearly from repo skills, or just sticks to the repo skills.

evals.name: route-orchestrator-misuse
evals.prompt: ok i'm gonna fire up orchestrator to redesign the dashboard ui in this app, the current one looks like every other shadcn admin
evals.expected_output: Pushes back: this isn't a multi-agent build. Recommends ui-brief first (to write the brief), then frontend-design or frontend-agent to build from it. Names skills in code. Does NOT invoke any skill — just names them.

evals.name: explain-specific-skill
evals.prompt: what does the contract-author skill actually do and when should i be using it vs writing types myself
evals.expected_output: Explains contract-author: produces machine-readable integration contracts (API/data/types/events) in orchestrator phase 4 before agents implement. When to use: multi-agent builds with shared boundaries. Mentions composes_with neighbors (contract-auditor, dependency-coordinator, orchestrator) and the bundled templates. Does not over-explain or pad.

evals.name: compose-graph-question
evals.prompt: what skills naturally work together with orchestrator? trying to understand the build pipeline before i kick one off
evals.expected_output: Reads orchestrator's composes_with / spawned_by relationships and the role agents it spawns. Returns a concise picture (text tree or short mermaid) showing: plan-builder → orchestrator → contract-author → role agents (backend/frontend/etc) → qe-agent gate. Skills named in code.
